title: assonia manaharica
intro: Assonia manaharica, also known as the forest mint or wild mint, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical regions, particularly in Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The leaves of the plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which have been reported to offer health benefits such as relieving stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
